title: Stop Bombing #Gaza, Rebuild With Oasis Plan: Manuel Hassassian (Palestinian Ambassador to Denmark):
description:
Interview conducted by Tim Rush from the Schiller Institute at the Advocacy Summit of the Middle East Churches for Peace in Washington, D.C. on March 19, 2025, where H.E. Prof. Dr. Manuel Hassassian was a keynote speaker. (This is the full interview. The first video we uploaded was missing the last half minute.)

Resumé: 
The current situation in Gaza following the resumption of massive Israeli bombardments it totally unacceptable. His message to the conference was that the church leaders and their governments have to put pressure on Israel to stop the war and return to peace talks. Violence begets violence. There is no military solution.

The ambassador said the Schiller Institute's Oasis Plan can play an imperative role in bridging the gaps through development, progress and building common ground. The Palestinian and Egyptian Gaza plan could be integrated with an Oasis Plan international dimension, as a neutral, scientific approach that would not be rejected by the Americans or Europeans. He said that he has spoken about the Oasis Plan at Schiller Institute conferences and in Palestine.

Tim Rush explained that the Schiller Institute has lobbied for the Oasis Plan on Capital Hill in Washington, as a means to create a future vision of economic benefits. In the 1960s, former President Eisenhower proposed a “Water for Peace” plan, which was supported by then-President Johnson. A week before the 1967 Israeli-Arab Six-Day War, the International Conference on Water for Peace was held in Washington on May 23-31, with delegates from 94 countries. But the project was derailed by the war.

In conclusion, H.E. Prof. Dr. Manuel Hassassian said that the war cannot be sustained, there must be peace based on mutual understanding and respect, and Palestinian independence.

Read the full transcript here: https://schillerinstitute.com/blog/2025/03/24/stop-the-bombing-rebuild-with-the-oasis-plan-palestinian-ambassador-to-denmark-dr-manuel-hassassian-gives-interview-to-schiller-institute/
Contact H.E. Prof. Dr. Manuel Hassassian: https://www.infopalestine.dk/
Contact the Churches for Middle East Peace: https://cmep.org/
